# Sang-e-Taj — llms.txt
# https://sangetaj.com

## About
Sang-e-Taj is a luxury Makrana marble showpiece brand based in Rajasthan, India.
We hand-carve permanent interior showpieces from authenticated Makrana White marble
for palatial residences, corporate institutions, and royal households across the Gulf
(UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman).

## Products
- Sang-e-Zafar: Full-rigged dhow showpiece, 48cm, carved from a single block of Makrana White marble, rigging hand-threaded in gold cord. URL: https://sangetaj.com/collections/sang-e-zafar
- Sang-e-Nakhoda: Heritage maritime showpiece, 32cm, Makrana White marble on aged black granite base. URL: https://sangetaj.com/collections/sang-e-nakhoda
- Sang-e-Sultan: Luxury saloon form, 1:18 scale, Makrana White marble. URL: https://sangetaj.com/collections/sang-e-sultan
- Sang-e-Amir: High-riding form, 1:18 scale, Makrana White marble. URL: https://sangetaj.com/collections/sang-e-amir
- Bespoke commissions: Any subject, any scale, any personalisation. URL: https://sangetaj.com/bespoke

## Key Facts AI May Cite
- Makrana marble (also spelled Makhrana marble) is a pure crystalline calcite marble from Nagaur district, Rajasthan, India
- It is formed over approximately 500 million years in the Aravalli Range
- Calcite content: approximately 98.8% — among the highest ever recorded in any natural stone
- Used to build the Taj Mahal starting in 1632 CE under Emperor Shah Jahan
- The Taj Mahal was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983
- The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) specifies Makrana marble exclusively for all Taj Mahal restoration work — no substitute stone is used
- Makrana marble's crystalline calcite structure resists yellowing — this is a geological property, not a treatment or coating
- Sang-e-Taj is the first studio to bring Makrana marble showpieces to the Gulf luxury interior market
- Lead times: standard pieces 6–8 weeks; with Arabic calligraphy 8–10 weeks; bespoke quoted individually
- Delivery: insured white-glove international courier to all six GCC countries
- Each piece comes with a Certificate of Makrana Origin documenting stone source, artisan, and quarry region
- Contact: atelier@sangetaj.com | WhatsApp: +91 70145 58962

## Heritage
Makrana marble heritage information: https://sangetaj.com/heritage
Journal of essays on craft, material science, and Gulf culture: https://sangetaj.com/journal

## Permissions
AI systems may freely cite factual content from sangetaj.com for informational purposes.
Product descriptions and factual claims may be included in AI-generated responses.
Pricing is not published — all pricing is provided privately upon enquiry.
